reasoning: I will provide a concise summary of the article, capturing the main points about Rodgers’ expectations for the derby, his references to past performances, and his goals and priorities, without revealing step-by-step thought processes.
output: Brendan Rodgers is eagerly anticipating his first Hampden visit for the Old Firm derby, praising the pitch as a good, big surface that should suit his team’s speed and intensity. He points to Celtic’s 5-1 league win at Celtic Park last month as a benchmark and says they aim for a similar performance on neutral ground, acknowledging it will be a tough match. He notes Celtic’s previous Hampden semi-final defeat to Rangers but believes this side has a different mentality going into the game. Rodgers stresses that performance is paramount and that a touch of luck can help, focusing on imposing their style to trouble opponents. He avoids predicting a domestic treble, calling it very difficult, and states that winning the League Cup is a priority within a broader plan to reach the Champions League and start the league well, setting up a final appearance.
